model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.0e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget on the full 50k training set, the current run only covers a small fraction of the data. Increasing batch size should expose the model to substantially more examples within the same step budget, and a modest LR increase keeps optimization progress from slowing when the batch grows.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.0e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.0e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last change suggests this workload is strongly data-limited under the fixed-step evaluator. Pushing batch size to 256 should let the model cover the full training set within 256 updates, and a proportional LR increase keeps the larger-batch regime from becoming too conservative.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization setup now looks substantially healthier, so the next likely gain is modest extra capacity rather than more schedule tuning. Adding one hidden layer keeps the design simple, preserves the successful batch/optimizer regime, and should improve representation power without a large rewrite.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.0e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.0e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest gains so far have come from processing more examples under the fixed 256-step budget. Moving to batch size 512 should push coverage well past two epochs while keeping the same simple training loop, and a moderate LR increase matches the larger-batch regime without changing the successful optimizer family.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5.0e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.0e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current regime has improved mainly by scaling optimization with larger effective data throughput, and the scheduler rollback confirms the rest of the setup is solid. A modest LR increase is the cleanest next probe to see whether this larger-batch AdamW configuration is still slightly underpowered within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.0e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5.0e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The last LR increase overshot slightly, so this candidate restores the stronger 4e-3 setting and tests whether regularization is now the limiting factor. With only a few effective epochs and no label noise, a lighter weight decay may preserve more fit while keeping the otherwise best-performing configuration intact.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current optimizer and regularization settings look close to tuned, and recent scalar changes are only yielding marginal gains. A modest width increase is the cleanest next capacity probe: it preserves the successful training recipe while giving the MLP more room to use the extra data coverage already unlocked by the large batch.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 4","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Width is still buying improvements, which suggests the model has not saturated its usable capacity under the current optimizer regime. Adding one more hidden block is a straightforward next test that keeps the successful large-batch setup intact while increasing representational depth at relatively low implementation complexity.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The extra depth was counterproductive, so this candidate restores the better three-layer network and spends the next trial on the dimension that has produced the biggest gains so far: example throughput under the fixed 256-step budget. Increasing batch size again is a cleaner use of the remaining budget than stacking more MLP blocks.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest improvements have consistently come from processing more data within the fixed 256-step budget, and the latest jump to 768 confirms that trend even after widening the network. Pushing batch size to 1024 is the cleanest next test of whether the verifier still rewards additional example coverage more than further architecture or optimizer tweaks.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5.0e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.0e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The batch-size scaling continues to improve validation loss, which changes the optimization regime enough that the earlier 5e-3 result at smaller batch is no longer decisive. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.0e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5.0e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher LR slightly overshot at batch size 1024, so this candidate restores the stronger 4e-3 setting and continues the regularization sweep in the same direction that already helped earlier. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The regularization sweep has largely plateaued, while batch-size increases have repeatedly delivered the largest loss reductions in this fixed-step setting. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling remains the dominant source of verified improvement, and the current 1536 setting still benefited substantially without needing any optimizer retuning. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 3072","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Every large batch-size increase so far has produced a verified loss improvement, and no other edit family has matched that payoff in this workload. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 6144","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The most reliable pattern in this run has been monotonic improvement from larger batches under the fixed 256-step budget, and the move to 4096 still helped despite the longer runtime.
